title: WooCommerce API keys should not be disclosed
description: WooCommerce is a WordPress e-commerce plugin powering millions of online stores. WooCommerce REST API keys consist of a Consumer Key and Consumer Secret. Exposing these credentials provides access to store orders, customers, products, and payment data.
recommendation: Remove WooCommerce API keys from source code. Store consumer key/secret in environment variables. Revoke at WooCommerce > Settings > Advanced > REST API.
impact: Access to e-commerce store data including customer PII, order history, payment information, and full store management capabilities.

checks.message: WooCommerce Consumer Secret detected (cs_ prefix). WooCommerce consumer secrets authenticate REST API requests providing read/write access to the entire WooCommerce store. This includes all customer records and purchase history, payment methods and order details, product catalog and inventory, coupon and discount codes, and webhook configurations. Revoke immediately at WooCommerce > Settings > Advanced > REST API.

WooCommerce Consumer Key detected (ck_ prefix). WooCommerce consumer keys identify the API user and are used together with the consumer secret for authentication. Exposing the consumer key alongside its secret (commonly found in the same configuration file) provides complete WooCommerce store access. Review surrounding code for the consumer secret.
WooCommerce consumer secret detected in variable assignment. WooCommerce store credentials provide full e-commerce access. Revoke at WooCommerce > Settings > Advanced > REST API.
